topic: batch cooking for the week — chilli recipe

turns.content: need a decent chilli recipe. im doing batch cooking on sunday so I can just reheat stuff through the week instead of eating cereal for dinner like some kind of feral student.

Good plan. Here's a solid batch chilli that freezes well:

**Ingredients** (makes about 6-8 portions):
- 1kg beef mince
- 2 onions, diced
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tins chopped tomatoes
- 2 tins kidney beans, drained
- 1 tin black beans, drained
- 2 tbsp tomato puree
- 2 tsp cumin
- 2 tsp smoked paprika
- 1 tsp chilli powder (more to taste)
- 1 beef stock cube
- Salt, pepper

Brown the mince, drain the fat, soften the onions and garlic, add everything else, simmer on low for at least an hour. Longer is better.

Want it spicier, milder, or any dietary tweaks?
thats roughly what ive done before aye. couple of questions though. is it worth doing it in the slow cooker or is that just making life more complicated for the same result.

Slow cooker works brilliantly for chilli -- arguably better than stovetop because the low, long cook breaks everything down more. Brown the mince and onions in a pan first (you still need that step), then dump everything into the slow cooker on low for 6-8 hours or high for 3-4.

The advantage is you can set it and forget it while you do other things on a Sunday. The result is usually richer and more developed in flavour. Only downside is it can end up a bit watery if you're not careful -- use slightly less liquid than you would on the stove.

aye thatll work. I can brown the mince before I go for a run and let it do its thing while im out. other question. dark chocolate. ive seen folk put it in chilli. is that actually good or is it one of those things people say to sound interesting.

It's actually good. Not a gimmick. About 20-30g of dark chocolate (70%+) stirred in near the end adds depth -- it doesn't taste chocolatey, it just rounds out the heat and makes the sauce richer. Think of it like adding a bit of Worcestershire sauce or soy sauce. You don't taste it specifically but something would be missing without it.

Don't overdo it though. A small amount works. Half a bar and you've made dessert.

right ill give it a go. worst case its ruined and I go back to cereal. also thinking of doing rice portions separately in the freezer. just microwave a portion of each and thats dinner in five minutes.

Rice freezes really well actually. Cook it, spread it out on a tray to cool quickly, then portion it into containers or freezer bags. Key thing is cooling it fast -- don't leave it sitting at room temperature for hours. Microwave with a splash of water and it comes back perfectly.

Some people also batch cook jacket potatoes for the same purpose. Bake them, cool them, freeze them, microwave when needed. Good alternative if you want variety through the week.

jacket potatoes is a shout actually. might do half and half. right so the plan is. sunday morning run. come back. brown the mince. slow cooker on for the afternoon. rice and potatoes in the gaps. thats me sorted for the week.

That's a very efficient Sunday. You'll come back from the run, spend maybe 45 minutes on active cooking, and then it's all just waiting. End of the day you've got a week's worth of dinners sorted. Beats cereal by a wide margin.
low bar but aye. cheers for the recipe. ill report back on the chocolate situation.
Looking forward to the chocolate verdict. Enjoy the batch cooking day.
